description: Cross-SDK canonical byte-pin vectors for the EATP-08 v1.1 algorithm identifier. The conformant wire encoding is a top-level `alg_id` string member; under JCS (RFC 8785) it serialises to `{"alg_id":"<token>"}`. Both kailash-py and kailash-rs MUST produce byte-identical `canonical_member` and identical `expected_sha256` for every registry token. kailash-py is the canonical AUTHOR (first SDK to implement EATP-08 v1.1); kailash-rs VENDORS this file byte-for-byte as part of its eatp-v1 adoption (#1315) per rules/cross-sdk-inspection.md Rule 4a. The `expected_sha256` is the SHA-256 of the UTF-8 `canonical_member` bytes (ASCII-only; serialize_for_signing uses ensure_ascii=True).

non_conformant.name: deprecated_pre_registry_literal_bare_top_level_string
non_conformant.decode_with_pre_adoption_witness: reject: unsupported-algorithm (a bare top-level-string literal is an unregistered token, NOT a D2d form; a witness MUST NOT rescue it)
non_conformant.reason: EATP-08 v1.1.1 / mint#26: a top-level alg_id string equal to the deprecated literal is registry-matched (§5.1 step 2), is not a registry token, and is rejected with unsupported-algorithm with or without a witness. The two D2d forms are the nested-object alg_id value and unsigned `algorithm` metadata (§4.5), never a bare top-level string. Preserves the §3.3 top-level-string-token invariant the anti-strip design (§4.4/V6) depends on.

non_conformant.name: nested_pre_registry_object
non_conformant.reason: pre-registry nested-object encoding; accepted only via the D2d witnessed legacy path, mapped to eatp-v1

non_conformant.name: missing_alg_id_post_adoption
non_conformant.decode_with_pre_adoption_witness: accept as eatp-v1 only when the record carries the pre-registry algorithm metadata + a pre-adoption witness
non_conformant.reason: EATP-08 §4.2 (D2b): a post-adoption record without alg_id MUST be rejected, NOT default-filled

non_conformant.name: unregistered_token
non_conformant.decode_with_pre_adoption_witness: reject: unsupported-algorithm (witness does not rescue a non-pre-registry unknown token)
non_conformant.reason: EATP-08 §3.3: an unregistered token MUST emit unsupported-algorithm and MUST NOT fall through to eatp-v1

conformance_vectors_note: Named V1-V9 coverage map for EATP-08 v1.1 §6 (`:274-321`). The #1316 acceptance bar is 'V4-V7 + V9 pass'. V1/V2 are the registry round-trips above; V3 is the chain-walk layer (SignedEnvelope/verify), NOT this alg-id-decode module; V8 is a non-runtime amendment-procedure obligation. Each acceptance-bar entry maps to a named behavioral test in tests/regression/test_eatp08_alg_id_canonical_vectors.py (the test_vN_* functions); the coverage-map test asserts every acceptance V-id has a named test. V6(i) (prior-v2 monotonic) is xfail-strict until Shard 3A lands the monotonic-upgrade-violation enforcer.

conformance_vectors.name: round-trip-v2-future
conformance_vectors.scenario: alg_id='eatp-v2.ml-dsa'; accept if supported, else unsupported-algorithm (this verifier does not implement v2 → reject, no fall-through to v1)

conformance_vectors.name: mixed-chain-walk
conformance_vectors.scenario: chain H->M->T, D1 at each chain_ref; v2-canonical on H -> chain-ref-canonical-form-mismatch
conformance_vectors.covered_by: NOT exercised by this alg-id-decode module — chain-walk lives in the SignedEnvelope/verify layer; chain-ref-canonical-form-mismatch is out of #1316 scope (journal 0003)

conformance_vectors.name: implicit-v1-pre-adoption
conformance_vectors.scenario: record WITHOUT alg_id (algorithm metadata only), Ed25519+SHA-256, chain head pre-adoption, witnessed §4.3 signed marker, no prior v2 in chain

conformance_vectors.name: implicit-v1-post-adoption
conformance_vectors.scenario: record WITHOUT alg_id, chain head post-adoption (no qualifying witness)

conformance_vectors.name: alg-id-strip-attack
conformance_vectors.scenario: legitimate v2 record, attacker strips alg_id and re-canonicalizes

sub_cases.scenario: no prior records, chain head post-adoption
no prior records, attacker-chosen pre-adoption date, no corroborating pre-adoption witness (fixture REQUIRED)

conformance_vectors.name: witnessed-adoption-marker
conformance_vectors.scenario: attacker tampers the local signed marker (marker_sig) to make a post-adoption stripped record look pre-adoption; the §4.3.2 detection rule MUST catch it

conformance_vectors.name: precedence-rule-survives-jcs-rename
conformance_vectors.scenario: amendment-procedure obligation (D3 rename hook) — a process gate at ratification time, NOT a runtime fixture
conformance_vectors.covered_by: EXCLUDED from the executable set per §6 (`:272`, `:313-315`)

conformance_vectors.name: pre-registry-explicit-form-accept
conformance_vectors.scenario: pre-registry explicit form (nested {algorithm:'ed25519+sha256'} OR unsigned algorithm metadata), chain head pre-adoption, witnessed signed marker, no prior registry-form in chain -> accept as eatp-v1, logged
